## @jgengine/core/format/distance

- `DistanceFormat` (interface): interface DistanceFormat έΑΦ Options for {@link formatDistance}.
- `DistanceUnit` (type): type DistanceUnit = "m" | "km" | "auto" έΑΦ Target unit for {@link formatDistance}: meters, kilometers, or `"auto"` (km past 1000m).
- `formatDistance` (function): function formatDistance(meters: number, options: DistanceFormat = {}): string έΑΦ Format a distance given in meters as a HUD-ready string, switching to km automatically past 1000m when `unit: "auto"`.

## @jgengine/core/format/duration

- `DurationFormat` (interface): interface DurationFormat έΑΦ Options for {@link formatDuration}.
- `formatDelta` (function): function formatDelta(seconds: number, decimals: 0 | 1 | 2 = 2): string έΑΦ Format a signed time gap as `+m:ss.ff` / `-m:ss.ff`, for race deltas and split times.
- `formatDuration` (function): function formatDuration(seconds: number, options: DurationFormat = {}): string έΑΦ Format a duration in seconds as a clock string (`m:ss`, `m:ss.ff`, or `h:mm:ss`), the shape every timer and racing HUD needs.
- `formatOrdinal` (function): function formatOrdinal(value: number): string έΑΦ English ordinal for a placement number: 1 έΗΤ "1st", 2 έΗΤ "2nd", 3 έΗΤ "3rd", 11 έΗΤ "11th".
- `padNumber` (function): function padNumber(value: number, width: number): string έΑΦ Left-pad a non-negative integer to `width` digits with leading zeros.

## @jgengine/core/format/speed

- `SpeedFormat` (interface): interface SpeedFormat έΑΦ Options for {@link formatSpeed}.
- `SpeedUnit` (type): type SpeedUnit = "kmh" | "mph" | "knots" | "ms" έΑΦ Target unit for {@link formatSpeed}: km/h, mph, knots, or raw m/s.
- `formatSpeed` (function): function formatSpeed(metersPerSecond: number, options: SpeedFormat = {}): string έΑΦ Format a speed given in meters/second as a HUD-ready string in km/h, mph, knots, or m/s έΑΦ the one conversion table every speedometer and telemetry readout should share.

## @jgengine/core/i18n/i18n

- `Catalog` (type): type Catalog = Readonly<Record<Locale, Messages>> έΑΦ All locales' message tables, keyed by locale.
- `I18n` (interface): interface I18n έΑΦ Runtime translator over a message {@link Catalog} έΑΦ active locale, lookup with fallback, interpolation, and pluralization.
- `I18nOptions` (interface): interface I18nOptions έΑΦ Configuration for {@link createI18n}.
- `Locale` (type): type Locale = string έΑΦ A locale tag (e.g. `"en"`, `"es"`, `"ja"`) έΑΦ a key into a {@link Catalog}.
- `Messages` (type): type Messages = Readonly<Record<string, string>> έΑΦ Flat message table for one locale: key έΗΤ template string with `{param}` slots.
- `TParams` (type): type TParams = Readonly<Record<string, string | number>> έΑΦ Interpolation values for a message template.
- `createI18n` (function): function createI18n(options: I18nOptions): I18n έΑΦ Create a translator over a message catalog: active-locale lookup with a fallback-locale chain, `{param}` interpolation, and `Intl.PluralRules`-based pluralization. Observable (`subscribe`) so React re-renders on `setLocale`; the catalog is caller-owned static data, the locale is the only state.
- `interpolate` (function): function interpolate(template: string, params?: TParams): string έΑΦ Replace `{name}` placeholders in `template` with `params[name]`. Unknown placeholders are left intact. Pure and locale-independent.

## @jgengine/core/render/lookPreset

- `CINEMATIC_POST_PROCESSING` (const): const CINEMATIC_POST_PROCESSING: PostProcessingConfig έΑΦ Tuned tone-map + bloom + gentle SSAO + vignette/grade stack έΑΦ the shipped-game post look.
- `CINEMATIC_SKY` (const): const CINEMATIC_SKY: SkyEnvironmentConfig έΑΦ A static day sky: a real dome plus the shell's view-following shadow-casting sun and hemisphere fill.
- `GameLookInput` (interface): interface GameLookInput έΑΦ Explicit graphics knobs a game may set; the preset only fills the ones left undefined.
- `LookPreset` (type): type LookPreset = "cinematic" | "flat" έΑΦ Named default-look preset composing the existing lighting/sky/fog/post knobs into one field. `"cinematic"` (the default when unset) draws a scene lit like a shipped game έΑΦ a real day sky with a view-following shadow-casting sun + hemisphere fill, a network-free image-based-lighting environment so PBR surfaces catch soft reflections, and a tuned tone-map/bloom/AO/vignette post stack. `"flat"` opts out of the sky/IBL/post rig to the bare ambient+directional default (pre-#773). The upgraded default primitive materials έΑΦ tuned roughness/metalness plus subtle procedural surface detail so un-modeled boxes/capsules stop reading as flat plastic έΑΦ apply under both presets.
- `ResolvedGameLook` (interface): interface ResolvedGameLook έΑΦ Concrete lighting/backdrop/post the shell renders after the preset has filled unset knobs.
- `resolveGameLook` (function): function resolveGameLook(input: GameLookInput): ResolvedGameLook έΑΦ Expand a game's `look` into concrete lighting/backdrop/post. The default is `"cinematic"`, so a scene reads lit-like-a-game out of the box; `"flat"` passes the explicit knobs through untouched. Anything the game authored wins έΑΦ the preset only fills unset knobs, and it never adds a sky when the world already owns one (so the sky's tuned sun/hemisphere serve as the lighting rig).

## @jgengine/core/render/postProcessing

- `AoConfig` (interface): interface AoConfig έΑΦ Ground-truth ambient occlusion stage έΑΦ darkens contact creases and cavities for depth.
- `BloomConfig` (interface): interface BloomConfig έΑΦ UnrealBloom stage έΑΦ soft HDR glow around bright pixels (sun, glints, emissive).
- `DofConfig` (interface): interface DofConfig έΑΦ Depth-of-field (bokeh) stage έΑΦ throws the fore/background out of focus around a focus distance.
- `GradeConfig` (interface): interface GradeConfig έΑΦ Final colour-grade stage: lift/gain/gamma, saturation, vignette, film grain έΑΦ applied in display space after tone mapping.
- `PostProcessingConfig` (interface): interface PostProcessingConfig έΑΦ Declarative post-processing chain (RenderPass έΗΤ AO έΗΤ Bloom έΗΤ tone-map output έΗΤ Grade). Present on a game means the shell mounts an `EffectComposer` and owns the render; absent means the renderer draws directly (unchanged). Each stage is a config object, `false` to skip, or omitted for its default. Pure data έΑΦ no three.js types leak into core.
- `STUDIO_STAGE_POST` (const): const STUDIO_STAGE_POST: PostProcessingConfig έΑΦ A cinematic "product shot" post preset έΑΦ the full chain on (contact-AO, soft bloom, a warm film grade with vignette + a touch of grain + chromatic aberration). Meant for a `StudioStage` where a single parametric asset is framed on a backdrop, so every studio reads shipped, not intern-tier. DoF is left off by default (it needs a per-scene focus distance); set `dof` to enable it.
- `ToneMappingMode` (type): type ToneMappingMode = "aces" | "agx" | "reinhard" | "cineon" | "linear" | "none" έΑΦ Renderer tone-mapping curve applied by the post chain's output stage.

## @jgengine/core/ui

- `AccessibilityState` (interface): interface AccessibilityState έΑΦ Serializable accessibility preferences a game reads to adapt its presentation.
- `AccessibilityStore` (interface): interface AccessibilityStore έΑΦ Observable, serializable accessibility-preferences store.
- `ActionCooldown` (interface): interface ActionCooldown έΑΦ Cooldown state for an action, expressed so a radial sweep or countdown can render directly.
- `ActionCost` (interface): interface ActionCost έΑΦ Headless view model for a contextual action collection έΑΦ the data layer behind an RTS command card, an ability action bar, a build menu, or a radial. It carries *what the buttons mean* (label, hotkey, cost, cooldown, disabled reasons, toggle state) with zero rendering opinion, so a game keeps layout, dimensions, hotkeys, catalog-id mapping, renderer, and chrome caller-owned. The React renderers in `@jgengine/react/actionHud` consume this; a game can swap either without forking the logic here.
- `ActionDef` (interface): interface ActionDef έΑΦ A caller-authored contextual action έΑΦ the DATA input to the model. Every field except `id` is optional so a build button, a stance toggle, and a cooldown ability all use the same shape. Catalog-id mapping (turning `icon`/`label` into art and copy) stays caller-owned.
- `ActionReason` (interface): interface ActionReason έΑΦ A single blocking reason: a stable machine `code` plus caller-facing `message`.
- `BUILT_IN_SETTING_CATEGORIES` (const): const BUILT_IN_SETTING_CATEGORIES: readonly BuiltInSettingCategory[] έΑΦ έγι undocumented

The upgraded default primitive materials έΑΦ tuned roughness/metalness plus subtle procedural surface detail so un-modeled boxes/capsules stop reading as flat plastic έΑΦ apply under both presets.

- `ModalRecord` (interface): interface ModalRecord έΑΦ Headless, serializable model for a stack of modal dialogs έΑΦ the data layer behind a pause menu, a "quit to menu?" confirmation, a level-up popup, or any blocking overlay a game raises over its scene. It carries an ordered stack of opaque {@link ModalRecord}s (push to open, pop/resolve to close) and nothing about how they look: the model NEVER interprets a record's `kind` or a resolution's `result` string έΑΦ those are free-form game vocabulary the React chrome in `@jgengine/react` renders. Observable ({@link ModalStack.subscribe}) and round-trips through a save ({@link ModalStack.snapshot}/{@link ModalStack.restore}). An injected clock powers optional per-modal auto-dismiss timers; leave those unset and the model is pure and clockless.
- `ModalResolution` (interface): interface ModalResolution έΑΦ The outcome of closing a modal via {@link ModalStack.resolve} (or an auto-dismiss) έΑΦ the closed record's identity plus the free-form `result` string the caller chose. Delivered to the `onResolve` option and stashed as {@link ModalStack.lastResolution}.

- `ModalStackEntry` (interface): interface ModalStackEntry έΑΦ A serialized stack entry: the opaque record plus the clock time it opened (for timer restore).
- `ModalStackOptions` (interface): interface ModalStackOptions έΑΦ Options for {@link createModalStack}.
- `ModalStackSnapshot` (interface): interface ModalStackSnapshot έΑΦ Serializable state έΑΦ the ordered entries (bottom-first) and the id counter. Plain JSON.

- `ObjectiveBannerOptions` (interface): interface ObjectiveBannerOptions έΑΦ Options for {@link createObjectiveBanner}.
- `ObjectiveBannerPhase` (type): type ObjectiveBannerPhase = "in" | "hold" | "out" έΑΦ Which part of its life a banner is in: flying `"in"`, holding centered, or fading `"out"`. Progress `0..1` (see {@link ObjectiveBannerView.progress}) runs within whichever phase is active έΑΦ the renderer maps phase + progress to opacity/scale/translate however it likes.
- `ObjectiveBannerSnapshot` (interface): interface ObjectiveBannerSnapshot έΑΦ Serializable state of the queue and the active banner, for save/restore.
- `ObjectiveBannerView` (interface): interface ObjectiveBannerView έΑΦ The banner to draw right now: its content, the game-owned `kind`, the current {@link ObjectiveBannerPhase}, and `progress` `0..1` through that phase. This object is pooled and overwritten on the next {@link ObjectiveBannerController.current} call έΑΦ read it, don't retain it.
- `PanelDef` (interface): interface PanelDef έΑΦ A caller-authored panel/window declaration έΑΦ the DATA input to the model. Only `id` is required so a bag, a character sheet, and a one-off dialog all share the shape.
- `PanelPosition` (interface): interface PanelPosition έΑΦ Headless, serializable model for a set of player-toggled windows/panels έΑΦ the data layer behind a WoW-style UI where B opens the bag, C the character sheet, and ESC closes the topmost window. It carries *which panels are open*, their focus/z stacking order, per-panel position overrides, and exclusive-group membership, with zero rendering opinion. The React chrome in `@jgengine/react/panels` consumes this; a game keeps layout, skin, keybinds, and window content caller-owned. Pure and immutable: every reducer returns a new state and never mutates its input.
- `PanelState` (interface): interface PanelState έΑΦ Serializable state for a set of panels: which are `open`, their `z` focus stack (higher renders on top), optional per-panel `pos` overrides, and the `meta` registry (group/closable) captured from the defs so the reducers below need no defs argument. Plain JSON έΑΦ persist and rehydrate it freely.
- `PhotoModeState` (interface): interface PhotoModeState έΑΦ Serializable photo-mode preferences a game reads to compose its capture UI.
- `PhotoModeStore` (interface): interface PhotoModeStore έΑΦ Observable photo-mode store έΑΦ genre-agnostic state a game binds its capture flow to.
- `PopoverOptions` (interface): interface PopoverOptions έΑΦ Options for {@link placePopover}.
- `PopoverPlacement` (interface): interface PopoverPlacement έΑΦ The resolved popover position έΑΦ the side it actually opened on and its clamped top-left.
- `PopoverSide` (type): type PopoverSide = "top" | "bottom" | "left" | "right" έΑΦ Which side of its anchor a popover opens toward.
- `PostProcessingConfig` (interface): interface PostProcessingConfig έΑΦ Declarative post-processing chain (RenderPass έΗΤ AO έΗΤ Bloom έΗΤ tone-map output έΗΤ Grade). Present on a game means the shell mounts an `EffectComposer` and owns the render; absent means the renderer draws directly (unchanged). Each stage is a config object, `false` to skip, or omitted for its default. Pure data έΑΦ no three.js types leak into core.
- `RadialArc` (interface): interface RadialArc έΑΦ Arc the slices span. Omit for a full wheel (index 0 at the top). A partial `sweep` (e.g. `Math.PI` for a bottom half, `Math.PI / 2` for a quarter) packs the slices evenly inside `[startAngle, startAngle + sweep]` έΑΦ the arc/quick-bar forms. Angles are radians from "up" (έΙΤY), clockwise.
- `RadialSlice` (interface): interface RadialSlice έΑΦ One wedge of a radial menu έΑΦ geometry for rendering a slice. Angles are radians from "up" (έΙΤY), clockwise.
- `RadialVectorOptions` (interface): interface RadialVectorOptions extends RadialArc έΑΦ Options for {@link radialIndexFromVector}.
- `ResolvedAction` (interface): interface ResolvedAction έΑΦ A resolved action view model έΑΦ availability computed once from cooldown, cost, `disabled`, and any caller reasons. `enabled` is the single truth a renderer gates interaction on; `reasons` is the ordered explanation (cooldown, then unmet costs, then caller reasons, then a generic disable).
- `SETTING_IDS` (const): const SETTING_IDS: { readonly masterVolume: "sound.master"; readonly graphicsQuality: "graphics.quality"; readonly graphicsShadows: "graphics.shadows"; readonly graphicsUiScale: "graphics.uiScale"; readonly touchStyle: "controls.touchStyle"; readonly touchJoystick: "controls.touchJoystick"; } έΑΦ έγι undocumented
- `STUDIO_STAGE_POST` (const): const STUDIO_STAGE_POST: PostProcessingConfig έΑΦ A cinematic "product shot" post preset έΑΦ the full chain on (contact-AO, soft bloom, a warm film grade with vignette + a touch of grain + chromatic aberration). Meant for a `StudioStage` where a single parametric asset is framed on a backdrop, so every studio reads shipped, not intern-tier. DoF is left off by default (it needs a per-scene focus distance); set `dof` to enable it.
- `ScreenMarkerLayout` (interface): interface ScreenMarkerLayout έΑΦ Where and how to draw one marker, from {@link layoutScreenMarker}.
- `ScreenMarkerOptions` (interface): interface ScreenMarkerOptions έΑΦ Options for {@link layoutScreenMarker}.
- `ScreenMarkerViewport` (interface): interface ScreenMarkerViewport έΑΦ The `{ width, height }` of the viewport the marker is laid out within (CSS px).
- `ScreenProjection` (interface): interface ScreenProjection έΑΦ A world position projected to screen space by the caller's camera. This is the minimal shape {@link layoutScreenMarker} consumes έΑΦ deliberately a structural subset of `@jgengine/react`'s `EntityScreenProjection` (and what its `ProjectEntity`/`useWorldProjection` produce), mirrored here because `core` imports nothing higher. Feed it straight from a worldέΗΤscreen projector.
- `SelectionFocusDirection` (type): type SelectionFocusDirection = "next" | "prev" | "first" | "last" έΑΦ A 1-D focus move within the selection member strip.
- `SelectionGroup` (interface): interface SelectionGroup έΑΦ A bucket of same-`kind` entities in a large selection έΑΦ the RTS "12 Marines" control chip.
- `SelectionView` (interface): interface SelectionView έΑΦ A resolved multi-selection view model. `primary` is the focused entity's full detail; `members` is the stable-order strip; `groups` buckets members by kind (count-desc, then label) for the portrait-overflow case; `grouped` is the hint that the selection is large enough to prefer groups over per-member portraits.
- `SelectionWindow` (interface): interface SelectionWindow έΑΦ A contiguous slice of members for virtualizing a large selection strip.
- `SettingCategory` (type): type SettingCategory = BuiltInSettingCategory | (string & {}) έΑΦ Built-in category ids keep autocomplete; any other string makes a fresh category.
- `SettingCategoryDef` (interface): interface SettingCategoryDef έΑΦ Declares or relabels/reorders a category tab; use it for a custom category or to reshape the built-ins.
- `SettingKind` (type): type SettingKind = "slider" | "toggle" | "select" έΑΦ έγι undocumented
- `SettingOption` (interface): interface SettingOption έΑΦ έγι undocumented
- `SettingValue` (type): type SettingValue = number | boolean | string έΑΦ έγι undocumented
- `SettingsActionDef` (interface): interface SettingsActionDef έΑΦ A game-state action (Restart, Quit to menu, έΑο) shown as rows in the first "Game" settings tab έΑΦ never a floating button or a rebindable key.
- `SettingsStore` (interface): interface SettingsStore έΑΦ έγι undocumented
- `SettingsSurface` (type): type SettingsSurface = "quick" έΑΦ `quick` shows compact on-screen volume/graphics buttons; `false` (default) mounts no engine trigger έΑΦ open the menu from your own UI with `<SettingsTrigger>` or `useSettings().open()`.
- `SettingsVariant` (type): type SettingsVariant = "panel" | "sheet" | "sidebar" | "fullscreen" έΑΦ The four themed settings layouts, chosen with `defineGame({ settings: { variant } })`. All read the game's `--jg-*` theme tokens.
- `StoredObjectiveBanner` (interface): interface StoredObjectiveBanner έΑΦ A persisted banner record έΑΦ all announcement fields resolved, plus id and schedule.
- `SummarizeSelectionOptions` (interface): interface SummarizeSelectionOptions έΑΦ Options for {@link summarizeSelection}.
- `SwingTargetInput` (interface): interface SwingTargetInput έΑΦ The current target, or the fields the bar needs from it.
- `TEXT_SCALE_MAX` (const): const TEXT_SCALE_MAX: 2 έΑΦ Maximum UI text scale.
- `TEXT_SCALE_MIN` (const): const TEXT_SCALE_MIN: 0.75 έΑΦ Minimum UI text scale.
- `TParams` (type): type TParams = Readonly<Record<string, string | number>> έΑΦ Interpolation values for a message template.
- `ToneMappingMode` (type): type ToneMappingMode = "aces" | "agx" | "reinhard" | "cineon" | "linear" | "none" έΑΦ Renderer tone-mapping curve applied by the post chain's output stage.
- `TooltipContent` (interface): interface TooltipContent έΑΦ Structured tooltip content έΑΦ a renderer lays these fields out; the model owns what is shown.
- `UI_SCALE_MAX` (const): const UI_SCALE_MAX: 1.5 έΑΦ έγι undocumented
- `UI_SCALE_MIN` (const): const UI_SCALE_MIN: 0.5 έΑΦ έγι undocumented
- `UiRect` (interface): interface UiRect έΑΦ A positioned rectangle (viewport/anchor space, top-left origin, pixels).
- `UiSize` (interface): interface UiSize έΑΦ A width/height pair (pixels).
- `Waypoint` (interface): interface Waypoint έΑΦ A tracked objective/waypoint the game wants a screen marker for.
- `WaypointSnapshot` (interface): interface WaypointSnapshot έΑΦ Serializable state of a {@link WaypointTracker}, for save/restore.
- `WaypointTracker` (interface): interface WaypointTracker έΑΦ An observable, serializable set of world waypoints keyed by id.
- `actionByHotkey` (function): function actionByHotkey(actions: readonly ResolvedAction[], key: string): string | null έΑΦ Find the action whose `hotkey` matches `key`, case-insensitively έΑΦ the keyboard-shortcut router for an action bar. Returns the first match's id, or null. Matching by hotkey does not consider `enabled`; the caller decides whether to fire a disabled action's feedback.
- `actionCooldown` (function): function actionCooldown(remainingMs: number, totalMs: number): ActionCooldown έΑΦ Build an {@link ActionCooldown} from remaining/total milliseconds. `ready` when nothing remains.
- `actionCooldownFromFraction` (function): function actionCooldownFromFraction(remainingMs: number, fraction: number): ActionCooldown έΑΦ Build an {@link ActionCooldown} from remaining milliseconds and a known remaining fraction έΑΦ the shape adapters get from sources (like an ability snapshot) that report a fraction but not a total.
- `actionCost` (function): function actionCost(resourceId: string, amount: number, available?: number): ActionCost έΑΦ Build an {@link ActionCost}. `met` is true unless `available` is provided and below `amount`.
- `actionTooltip` (function): function actionTooltip(action: ResolvedAction): TooltipContent έΑΦ Build {@link TooltipContent} from a {@link ResolvedAction}: title from the label, the group as a subtitle, the description as the body, and the blocking reasons as warning notes. The single place an action's tooltip copy is assembled, so every renderer shows the same thing.
- `busVolumeSettingId` (function): function busVolumeSettingId(busId: string): string έΑΦ έγι undocumented
- `clampTextScale` (function): function clampTextScale(scale: number): number έΑΦ Clamp a text-scale multiplier into the supported `[TEXT_SCALE_MIN, TEXT_SCALE_MAX]` range.
- `closePanel` (function): function closePanel(state: PanelState, id: string): PanelState έΑΦ Close panel `id`. No-op if it is already closed. Its position override is kept so reopening restores the last placement.
- `closeTopPanel` (function): function closeTopPanel(state: PanelState): PanelState έΑΦ Close the topmost closable open panel έΑΦ the ESC handler. Skips panels pinned with `closable: false` and returns the same state when nothing closable is open.
- `createAccessibilityStore` (function): function createAccessibilityStore(initial: Partial<AccessibilityState> = {}): AccessibilityStore έΑΦ Serializable, observable store of accessibility preferences (reduced motion, high contrast, text scale, colorblind mode, captions). Genre-agnostic plumbing like input rebinding έΑΦ a game binds these to its settings UI and adapts its presentation; `snapshot`/`restore` round-trip through a save or settings blob.
- `createCoachMarkSequence` (function): function createCoachMarkSequence(options: CoachMarkSequenceOptions): CoachMarkSequence έΑΦ An onboarding coach-mark run: an ordered sequence of hint steps, each shown as a centered callout or anchored to a UI element, with a persisted "seen" set so completed onboarding stays done across sessions, and data-first condition gating so a step waits until its trigger id is satisfied. Advance/skip/dismiss drive it; `snapshot`/`restore` round-trip the seen set through a save. Purely a model έΑΦ a React host renders `current()`.
- `createI18n` (function): function createI18n(options: I18nOptions): I18n έΑΦ Create a translator over a message catalog: active-locale lookup with a fallback-locale chain, `{param}` interpolation, and `Intl.PluralRules`-based pluralization. Observable (`subscribe`) so React re-renders on `setLocale`; the catalog is caller-owned static data, the locale is the only state.
- `createModalStack` (function): function createModalStack(options: ModalStackOptions = {}): ModalStack έΑΦ A stack of modal dialogs: push opaque records to open blocking overlays (a pause menu, a confirm prompt, a reward popup), pop or resolve them with a free-form result to close, and query the top / open / depth to gate game input. The model is deliberately vocabulary-free έΑΦ it never reads a record's `kind` or a resolution's `result`, so a game owns every dialog style έΑΦ and fully serializable, with an optional injected clock driving per-modal auto-dismiss. A React host renders `top()`; `subscribe` re-renders it, `snapshot`/`restore` round-trip it through a save.
- `createObjectiveBanner` (function): function createObjectiveBanner(options: ObjectiveBannerOptions = {}): ObjectiveBannerController έΑΦ A serializable, clock-driven stage/objective banner controller: the classic transient centered title stamp ("WAVE 3", "VICTORY", "OBJECTIVE COMPLETE"). A game calls `announce({ title, subtitle?, kind? })` and the controller queues it, flies it in, holds it, and fades it out on an injected clock έΑΦ one banner at a time έΑΦ exposing the current banner plus its phase (in/hold/out) and `progress` `0..1` for a renderer to animate opacity/scale/translate. Nothing here is genre-specific: `kind` is a free label the game owns and styles, and all timings are parameters. Call `advance()` each frame; `current()` returns the pooled view to draw; `snapshot`/`restore` round-trips the queue through a save.
- `createPanelState` (function): function createPanelState(defs: readonly PanelDef[]): PanelState έΑΦ Build the initial {@link PanelState} from the panel defs: panels flagged `initial` start open (in declaration order, respecting group exclusivity), and every def's group/closable is captured into the state so later reducers need no defs.
- `createPhotoModeStore` (function): function createPhotoModeStore(initial: Partial<PhotoModeState> = {}): PhotoModeStore έΑΦ Serializable, observable photo-mode state (active + hide-HUD). Genre-agnostic plumbing έΑΦ a game engages it to free its camera and hide gameplay chrome for a clean capture, and reads `hideHud` to conditionally render its HUD. Pair with React `PhotoModeControls` and shell `captureCanvas`.
- `createSettingsStore` (function): function createSettingsStore(storage: Pick<WebStorageLike, "getItem" | "setItem"> | null | undefined = defaultStorage()): SettingsStore έΑΦ Reactive, localStorage-backed settings store shared by the shell wiring and React hooks.
- `createWaypointTracker` (function): function createWaypointTracker(): WaypointTracker έΑΦ A serializable, observable tracker of world waypoints έΑΦ the objective/point-of- interest half of offscreen markers. A game `set`s waypoints by id (each an `{ id, position, kind, label? }`, where `kind` is a free string the game styles and the model never branches on), and a React host projects each to screen space and lays it out with {@link layoutScreenMarker}: an on-screen pin, or an off-screen directional arrow clamped to the viewport edge with a distance the caller computes. Purely a model έΑΦ no camera, projection, or renderer here. `snapshot`/`restore` round-trips the set through a save.
- `focusPanel` (function): function focusPanel(state: PanelState, id: string): PanelState έΑΦ Raise open panel `id` to the top of the focus stack (assign it the highest z). No-op if it is closed or already on top, so a pointer-down that refocuses the top window returns the same state.
- `formatDelta` (function): function formatDelta(seconds: number, decimals: 0 | 1 | 2 = 2): string έΑΦ Format a signed time gap as `+m:ss.ff` / `-m:ss.ff`, for race deltas and split times.
- `formatDistance` (function): function formatDistance(meters: number, options: DistanceFormat = {}): string έΑΦ Format a distance given in meters as a HUD-ready string, switching to km automatically past 1000m when `unit: "auto"`.
- `formatDuration` (function): function formatDuration(seconds: number, options: DurationFormat = {}): string έΑΦ Format a duration in seconds as a clock string (`m:ss`, `m:ss.ff`, or `h:mm:ss`), the shape every timer and racing HUD needs.
- `formatOrdinal` (function): function formatOrdinal(value: number): string έΑΦ English ordinal for a placement number: 1 έΗΤ "1st", 2 έΗΤ "2nd", 3 έΗΤ "3rd", 11 έΗΤ "11th".
- `formatSpeed` (function): function formatSpeed(metersPerSecond: number, options: SpeedFormat = {}): string έΑΦ Format a speed given in meters/second as a HUD-ready string in km/h, mph, knots, or m/s έΑΦ the one conversion table every speedometer and telemetry readout should share.
- `hudScaleForViewport` (function): function hudScaleForViewport(fit: Required<HudFitConfig>, viewport: HudSize): number έΑΦ The one scaling rule for every display: the ratio of the live viewport to the authored design size along the limiting axis, clamped. 1 on a viewport at or above design size; smoothly below 1 down to `minScale` on phones.
- `interpolate` (function): function interpolate(template: string, params?: TParams): string έΑΦ Replace `{name}` placeholders in `template` with `params[name]`. Unknown placeholders are left intact. Pure and locale-independent.
- `isOpen` (function): function isOpen(state: PanelState, id: string): boolean έΑΦ Whether panel `id` is currently open.
- `layoutScreenMarker` (function): function layoutScreenMarker(projection: ScreenProjection, viewport: ScreenMarkerViewport, options: ScreenMarkerOptions = {}): ScreenMarkerLayout έΑΦ Pure, allocation-aware screen-marker math: given a projected target point, the viewport, and a `margin`, decide whether the target is on-screen and where to draw its marker. An on-screen point passes straight through (`onScreen: true`). An off-screen έΑΦ or behind-camera έΑΦ point is CLAMPED to the viewport edge (inset by `margin`) with `angle` set to the bearing from screen center toward the target, so a caller can pin an arrow to the frame edge and rotate it to point the way. Behind-camera projections have mirrored coordinates, so the bearing is flipped to face the true direction. This is the edge-clamp/arrow half that `layoutEntityFrames` lacks (it culls off-screen entries instead). No allocation when an `out` object is supplied έΑΦ reuse one across a whole frame's waypoints.
- `listHudPanelTypes` (function): function listHudPanelTypes(): HudPanelTypeDef[] έΑΦ Every registered panel type, sorted by id έΑΦ editor palette / agent listing.
- `moveGridFocus` (function): function moveGridFocus(current: number, count: number, columns: number, dir: FocusDirection, options?: GridFocusOptions): number έΑΦ Pure grid focus math for keyboard and controller navigation of an action card laid out in `columns`-wide rows. Given the current index (`-1` for none) it returns the next index, clamped to `[0, count)` έΑΦ or wrapped when `wrap` is set. `left/right` move within/across columns, `up/down` across rows, `next/prev` walk the flat order, `first/last` jump to the ends. Renderer-agnostic: radial and list layouts pass `columns: 1` (or `count`) to get sensible 1-D stepping.
- `movePanel` (function): function movePanel(state: PanelState, id: string, pos: PanelPosition): PanelState έΑΦ Set panel `id`'s position override έΑΦ the drag commit. Does not require the panel to be open, so a caller can pre-place a window before it is shown.
- `moveSelectionFocus` (function): function moveSelectionFocus(count: number, current: number, dir: SelectionFocusDirection): number έΑΦ Pure next-focus math for the selection strip: step the focus index by one member (wrapping), or jump to an end. `-1` (no focus) steps to the first/last member. Returns `-1` for an empty selection.
- `openPanel` (function): function openPanel(state: PanelState, id: string): PanelState έΑΦ Open panel `id`, raising it to the top of the focus stack. If it belongs to a `group`, the other open panels in that group are closed (exclusive windows). Opening an already-open panel just refocuses it. Position overrides are preserved.
- `orderedOpen` (function): function orderedOpen(state: PanelState, defs: readonly PanelDef[]): PanelDef[] έΑΦ The open panels' defs sorted ascending by z έΑΦ the render order for a window host (map to elements so the last, highest-z window paints on top). Defs unknown to the state are skipped.
- `orientationGateActive` (function): function orientationGateActive(requirement: OrientationRequirement, liveOrientation: LayoutOrientation): boolean έΑΦ The rotate gate blocks gameplay: a hard requirement (or `unsupported`) the live orientation doesn't satisfy.
- `orientationHintActive` (function): function orientationHintActive(requirement: OrientationRequirement, liveOrientation: LayoutOrientation): boolean έΑΦ An advisory rotate hint applies: a preference (not a hard gate) the live orientation doesn't satisfy.
- `overflowingPanels` (function): function overflowingPanels(panels: readonly { id: string; rect: HudRect }[], viewport: HudSize, tolerance = 1.5): HudOverflow[] έΑΦ Every panel rect that escapes the viewport έΑΦ the data behind the HUD overflow gate. A rect is only reported when it is partly visible *and* crosses an edge; a fully off-screen (closed/hidden) surface is skipped, so parked drawers and unopened menus never raise a false alarm.
- `panelByHotkey` (function): function panelByHotkey(defs: readonly PanelDef[], code: string): string | null έΑΦ Find the panel whose `hotkey` matches `code`, case-insensitively έΑΦ the keybind router. `code` may be a `KeyboardEvent.code` (`"KeyB"`) or key (`"b"`); it is compared verbatim to each def's `hotkey`, so author the hotkey in whichever form the caller feeds in. Returns the first match's id, or null.
- `placePopover` (function): function placePopover(anchor: UiRect, content: UiSize, viewport: UiSize, options?: PopoverOptions): PopoverPlacement έΑΦ Position a popover/tooltip of `content` size against its `anchor` rectangle inside `viewport`: open on the `preferred` side, flip to the opposite when that side lacks room and the opposite has more, then clamp the top-left so the box stays fully on screen (respecting `margin`). Pure and deterministic έΑΦ no DOM reads έΑΦ so it is SSR-safe and unit-testable, and the React tooltip is a thin shell over it.
- `radialIndexFromAngle` (function): function radialIndexFromAngle(angle: number, count: number, arc?: RadialArc): number έΑΦ Map an angle to a slice index; returns -1 when the angle falls outside a partial `arc`.
- `radialIndexFromVector` (function): function radialIndexFromVector(dx: number, dy: number, count: number, options: RadialVectorOptions = {}): number | null έΑΦ Pick the slice a pointer/stick vector points at. `dx`/`dy` are screen-space (y down): up is `(0, -1)`. Returns null inside `deadZone` or (for a partial `arc`) outside the arc, so a centered stick or a click off the arc selects nothing.
- `radialSlicePosition` (function): function radialSlicePosition(index: number, count: number, radius: number, arc?: RadialArc): { x: number; y: number } έΑΦ Unit-circle position of a slice center (up = `(0, -1)`), scaled by `radius` έΑΦ where to place a wedge's icon/label.
- `radialSlices` (function): function radialSlices(count: number, arc?: RadialArc): RadialSlice[] έΑΦ Divide a ring (or `arc`) into `count` wedges έΑΦ the geometry a radial/quick menu (weapon wheel, emote arc, ping bar) renders. A full wheel centers index 0 at the top; a partial `arc` packs the slices inside its sweep.
- `reducedMotionDuration` (function): function reducedMotionDuration(state: AccessibilityState, ms: number, reduced = 0): number έΑΦ Return `ms` when motion is allowed, or `reduced` (default 0) when `reducedMotion` is set έΑΦ the animation-duration gate.
- `registerHudPanelType` (function): function registerHudPanelType(def: HudPanelTypeDef): void έΑΦ Register a HUD panel type so the editor can list/add/configure it and canvas resize knows axes.
- `resizePanelSize` (function): function resizePanelSize(current: HudSize, delta: { dw: number; dh: number }, axes: HudResizeAxes, limits?: { minWidth?: number; maxWidth?: number; minHeight?: number; maxHeight?: number; }): HudSize έΑΦ Semantic resize: apply pixel deltas only on growable axes, clamped to min/max. Never scales content έΑΦ callers reflow layout size (track length, list rows).
- `resolveAction` (function): function resolveAction(def: ActionDef): ResolvedAction έΑΦ Resolve one {@link ActionDef} into a {@link ResolvedAction}: normalize label, compute `enabled`, and order the blocking `reasons`. Pure έΑΦ the same input always yields the same view model.
- `resolveActionCollection` (function): function resolveActionCollection(defs: readonly ActionDef[]): ResolvedAction[] έΑΦ Resolve an ordered list of actions, preserving input order.
- `resolveGameLook` (function): function resolveGameLook(input: GameLookInput): ResolvedGameLook έΑΦ Expand a game's `look` into concrete lighting/backdrop/post. The default is `"cinematic"`, so a scene reads lit-like-a-game out of the box; `"flat"` passes the explicit knobs through untouched. Anything the game authored wins έΑΦ the preset only fills unset knobs, and it never adds a sky when the world already owns one (so the sky's tuned sun/hemisphere serve as the lighting rig).
- `resolveHudFit` (function): function resolveHudFit(config: HudViewportConfig | undefined, mobile: boolean): Required<HudFitConfig> έΑΦ έγι undocumented
- `resolveHudPanelLayout` (function): function resolveHudPanelLayout(docPanel: EditorUiPanelLayout | undefined, fallback: HudPanelFallback): ResolvedHudPanelLayout έΑΦ Resolve a panel's layout: document entry wins field-by-field over TSX fallback. TSX props are fallback-only έΑΦ the scene document is the source of truth once authored.
- `resolveOrientationRequirement` (function): function resolveOrientationRequirement(orientation: GameOrientation | undefined, platform: "mobile" | "desktop"): OrientationRequirement έΑΦ Resolve the game's orientation declaration into a concrete requirement for a platform. Desktop is always unconstrained.
- `selectionWindow` (function): function selectionWindow(members: readonly EntitySummaryDef[], scroll: number, size: number): SelectionWindow έΑΦ Compute a virtualization window over a large selection: a `size`-length slice starting near `scroll`, clamped so the window never runs past the end. Render only `items`, offsetting by `start * itemSize`, to keep a thousand-unit selection from mounting a thousand portraits.
- `summarizeSelection` (function): function summarizeSelection(members: readonly EntitySummaryDef[], options?: SummarizeSelectionOptions): SelectionView έΑΦ Summarize a selected-entity list into a {@link SelectionView}: resolve the primary focus, keep the ordered members, and bucket them by `kind`. Pure and stable έΑΦ deterministic group ordering and a clamped focus index, so it is safe to call every render and to assert on in a test.
- `swingTimerState` (function): function swingTimerState(player: SwingPlayerInput, target: SwingTargetInput | null, prevPeriod: number, prevTimer: number): SwingTimerState έΑΦ Pure swing-timer bar state έΑΦ no hidden state, no clock, no DOM. The caller threads `prevPeriod`/`prevTimer` back each frame. The period is recovered on the reset edge (when `swingTimer` jumps up = a new swing began) as `max(swingTimer, weapon.speed)`, so the fill is correct even without knowing the weapon's exact cadence. Hidden unless auto-attacking a live, non-object target.
- `togglePanel` (function): function togglePanel(state: PanelState, id: string): PanelState έΑΦ Toggle panel `id` έΑΦ open it (with group exclusivity) if closed, close it if open. The one-key behavior behind a keybind like `B` for the bag.

## @jgengine/core/ui/accessibility

- `AccessibilityState` (interface): interface AccessibilityState έΑΦ Serializable accessibility preferences a game reads to adapt its presentation.
- `AccessibilityStore` (interface): interface AccessibilityStore έΑΦ Observable, serializable accessibility-preferences store.
- `COLORBLIND_MATRICES` (const): const COLORBLIND_MATRICES: Record<ColorblindMode, string | null> έΑΦ `feColorMatrix` `values` strings for each colorblind mode έΑΦ simulate how a viewer with that condition perceives color (and a grayscale mode). `"none"` maps to `null` (no filter). Consumed by the React `ColorblindFilters` defs.
- `ColorblindMode` (type): type ColorblindMode = "none" | "protanopia" | "deuteranopia" | "tritanopia" | "grayscale" έΑΦ Colorblind simulation/daltonization modes the engine ships matrices for.
- `DEFAULT_ACCESSIBILITY` (const): const DEFAULT_ACCESSIBILITY: AccessibilityState έΑΦ The out-of-the-box accessibility state (all assistance off, `textScale` 1).
- `TEXT_SCALE_MAX` (const): const TEXT_SCALE_MAX: 2 έΑΦ Maximum UI text scale.
- `TEXT_SCALE_MIN` (const): const TEXT_SCALE_MIN: 0.75 έΑΦ Minimum UI text scale.
- `clampTextScale` (function): function clampTextScale(scale: number): number έΑΦ Clamp a text-scale multiplier into the supported `[TEXT_SCALE_MIN, TEXT_SCALE_MAX]` range.
- `createAccessibilityStore` (function): function createAccessibilityStore(initial: Partial<AccessibilityState> = {}): AccessibilityStore έΑΦ Serializable, observable store of accessibility preferences (reduced motion, high contrast, text scale, colorblind mode, captions). Genre-agnostic plumbing like input rebinding έΑΦ a game binds these to its settings UI and adapts its presentation; `snapshot`/`restore` round-trip through a save or settings blob.
- `reducedMotionDuration` (function): function reducedMotionDuration(state: AccessibilityState, ms: number, reduced = 0): number έΑΦ Return `ms` when motion is allowed, or `reduced` (default 0) when `reducedMotion` is set έΑΦ the animation-duration gate.

## @jgengine/core/ui/actionModel

- `ActionCooldown` (interface): interface ActionCooldown έΑΦ Cooldown state for an action, expressed so a radial sweep or countdown can render directly.
- `ActionCost` (interface): interface ActionCost έΑΦ Headless view model for a contextual action collection έΑΦ the data layer behind an RTS command card, an ability action bar, a build menu, or a radial. It carries *what the buttons mean* (label, hotkey, cost, cooldown, disabled reasons, toggle state) with zero rendering opinion, so a game keeps layout, dimensions, hotkeys, catalog-id mapping, renderer, and chrome caller-owned. The React renderers in `@jgengine/react/actionHud` consume this; a game can swap either without forking the logic here.
- `ActionDef` (interface): interface ActionDef έΑΦ A caller-authored contextual action έΑΦ the DATA input to the model. Every field except `id` is optional so a build button, a stance toggle, and a cooldown ability all use the same shape. Catalog-id mapping (turning `icon`/`label` into art and copy) stays caller-owned.
- `ActionReason` (interface): interface ActionReason έΑΦ A single blocking reason: a stable machine `code` plus caller-facing `message`.
- `FocusDirection` (type): type FocusDirection = "left" | "right" | "up" | "down" | "next" | "prev" | "first" | "last" έΑΦ A focus move for keyboard/controller grid navigation over an action collection.
- `GridFocusOptions` (interface): interface GridFocusOptions έΑΦ Options for {@link moveGridFocus}.
- `ResolvedAction` (interface): interface ResolvedAction έΑΦ A resolved action view model έΑΦ availability computed once from cooldown, cost, `disabled`, and any caller reasons. `enabled` is the single truth a renderer gates interaction on; `reasons` is the ordered explanation (cooldown, then unmet costs, then caller reasons, then a generic disable).
- `actionByHotkey` (function): function actionByHotkey(actions: readonly ResolvedAction[], key: string): string | null έΑΦ Find the action whose `hotkey` matches `key`, case-insensitively έΑΦ the keyboard-shortcut router for an action bar. Returns the first match's id, or null. Matching by hotkey does not consider `enabled`; the caller decides whether to fire a disabled action's feedback.
- `actionCooldown` (function): function actionCooldown(remainingMs: number, totalMs: number): ActionCooldown έΑΦ Build an {@link ActionCooldown} from remaining/total milliseconds. `ready` when nothing remains.
- `actionCooldownFromFraction` (function): function actionCooldownFromFraction(remainingMs: number, fraction: number): ActionCooldown έΑΦ Build an {@link ActionCooldown} from remaining milliseconds and a known remaining fraction έΑΦ the shape adapters get from sources (like an ability snapshot) that report a fraction but not a total.
- `actionCost` (function): function actionCost(resourceId: string, amount: number, available?: number): ActionCost έΑΦ Build an {@link ActionCost}. `met` is true unless `available` is provided and below `amount`.
- `moveGridFocus` (function): function moveGridFocus(current: number, count: number, columns: number, dir: FocusDirection, options?: GridFocusOptions): number έΑΦ Pure grid focus math for keyboard and controller navigation of an action card laid out in `columns`-wide rows. Given the current index (`-1` for none) it returns the next index, clamped to `[0, count)` έΑΦ or wrapped when `wrap` is set. `left/right` move within/across columns, `up/down` across rows, `next/prev` walk the flat order, `first/last` jump to the ends. Renderer-agnostic: radial and list layouts pass `columns: 1` (or `count`) to get sensible 1-D stepping.
- `resolveAction` (function): function resolveAction(def: ActionDef): ResolvedAction έΑΦ Resolve one {@link ActionDef} into a {@link ResolvedAction}: normalize label, compute `enabled`, and order the blocking `reasons`. Pure έΑΦ the same input always yields the same view model.
- `resolveActionCollection` (function): function resolveActionCollection(defs: readonly ActionDef[]): ResolvedAction[] έΑΦ Resolve an ordered list of actions, preserving input order.

## @jgengine/core/ui/coachMarks

- `CoachMarkPlacement` (type): type CoachMarkPlacement = "top" | "bottom" | "left" | "right" | "center" έΑΦ Where a coach-mark callout sits relative to its anchor (or `"center"` for an unanchored callout).
- `CoachMarkSequence` (interface): interface CoachMarkSequence έΑΦ An ordered, observable, serializable onboarding coach-mark run.
- `CoachMarkSequenceOptions` (interface): interface CoachMarkSequenceOptions έΑΦ Options for {@link createCoachMarkSequence}.
- `CoachMarkSnapshot` (interface): interface CoachMarkSnapshot έΑΦ Serializable persisted state έΑΦ the seen set (with timestamps) and satisfied triggers.
- `CoachMarkStep` (interface): interface CoachMarkStep έΑΦ One onboarding hint: an ordered, optionally-anchored, optionally-gated callout.
- `CoachMarkView` (interface): interface CoachMarkView έΑΦ The active step plus its position, returned by {@link CoachMarkSequence.current}.
- `createCoachMarkSequence` (function): function createCoachMarkSequence(options: CoachMarkSequenceOptions): CoachMarkSequence έΑΦ An onboarding coach-mark run: an ordered sequence of hint steps, each shown as a centered callout or anchored to a UI element, with a persisted "seen" set so completed onboarding stays done across sessions, and data-first condition gating so a step waits until its trigger id is satisfied. Advance/skip/dismiss drive it; `snapshot`/`restore` round-trip the seen set through a save. Purely a model έΑΦ a React host renders `current()`.

## @jgengine/core/ui/floatingText

- `FloatingTextDef` (interface): interface FloatingTextDef έΑΦ One text pop to spawn: what to say, where in the world, and how it moves and fades. Everything but `position` and `text` is optional and falls back to the field's defaults. `kind` is a free-form tag (`"damage"`, `"heal"`, `"crit"`, `"xp"`, `"gold"`, `"bark"`, έΑο) the presentation layer styles έΑΦ the model never interprets it, so nothing here is combat- or genre-specific.
- `FloatingTextField` (interface): interface FloatingTextField έΑΦ A live, dt-driven field of world-anchored floating text.
- `FloatingTextFieldConfig` (interface): interface FloatingTextFieldConfig έΑΦ Construction options for {@link createFloatingTextField}.
- `FloatingTextPosition` (type): type FloatingTextPosition = readonly [number, number, number] έΑΦ A world-space anchor `[x, y, z]` a floating-text entry rises from.
- `FloatingTextSnapshot` (interface): interface FloatingTextSnapshot έΑΦ Serializable state for save/restore and deterministic replay.
- `FloatingTextView` (interface): interface FloatingTextView έΑΦ A live entry resolved for rendering: current world position and fade/scale over life.
- `createFloatingTextField` (function): function createFloatingTextField(config: FloatingTextFieldConfig = {}): FloatingTextField έΑΦ A generic, allocation-aware field of world-anchored floating text: damage numbers, heals, crits, XP/gold gains, status pops, dialogue barks έΑΦ anything a game wants to say at a point in the world for a moment. It is dt-driven (call `update(dt)` each frame) and deterministic: the only randomness is the horizontal drift jitter, which flows from the injected `seed`, so the same seed and the same emit/update sequence reproduce identical motion, and `snapshot`/`restore` round-trips the live pool. The model owns only the spawn έΗΤ rise/drift έΗΤ fade έΗΤ reap lifecycle: `kind` is a free string and `color` a passthrough hint, so nothing here couples to combat or any genre. Presentation (projecting each entry to the screen and styling it per `kind`) lives in `@jgengine/react`'s `FloatingText`.

## @jgengine/core/ui/gameLayout

- `DetectCollisionsOptions` (interface): interface DetectCollisionsOptions έΑΦ Options for `detectLayoutCollisions`.
- `GameLayoutMode` (type): type GameLayoutMode = "desktop-wide" | "desktop-compact" | "mobile-landscape" | "mobile-portrait" έΑΦ The explicit composition mode a game renders for έΑΦ not a scaled desktop layout.
- `GameViewportLayout` (interface): interface GameViewportLayout έΑΦ The shared live geometry the engine allocates once and every UI subsystem reads.
- `HudPriority` (type): type HudPriority = "critical" | "secondary" | "tertiary" έΑΦ Gameplay-importance tier of a HUD element.
- `Insets` (interface): interface Insets έΑΦ Edge insets in CSS pixels (safe areas, reservations).
- `LayoutCollision` (interface): interface LayoutCollision έΑΦ One detected forbidden/warned overlap between two regions.
- `LayoutCollisionPolicy` (type): type LayoutCollisionPolicy = "forbid" | "allow" | "warn" έΑΦ How a region participates in collision reporting.
- `LayoutModeInput` (interface): interface LayoutModeInput έΑΦ Inputs to `resolveLayoutMode`.
- `LayoutRect` (interface): interface LayoutRect έΑΦ Axis-aligned rectangle in CSS pixels (origin top-left). Structurally compatible with a `DOMRect`'s edge fields.
- `LayoutRegion` (interface): interface LayoutRegion έΑΦ A physical rectangle a UI subsystem occupies, published to the shared registry.
- `LayoutRegionKind` (type): type LayoutRegionKind = "hud" | "control" | "system" | "screen" έΑΦ What kind of UI a registered region belongs to.
- `MobileHudBehavior` (type): type MobileHudBehavior = | "persistent" | "compact" | "icon" | "transient" | "hidden" | "sheet" | "modal" έΑΦ How a HUD element adapts on phones.
- `ZERO_INSETS` (const): const ZERO_INSETS: Insets έΑΦ All-zero insets.

## @jgengine/core/ui/hudDocument

- `EditorUiDocument` (interface): interface EditorUiDocument έΑΦ Scene-document HUD section: panel id έΗΤ layout. Single source of truth for placement.
- `EditorUiPanelLayout` (interface): interface EditorUiPanelLayout έΑΦ Authored layout for one HUD panel inside `editor.scene.json` έΗΤ `ui.panels`.
- `HudPanelFallback` (interface): interface HudPanelFallback έΑΦ TSX-declared fallback when the document has no entry for a panel.
- `HudPanelTypeDef` (interface): interface HudPanelTypeDef έΑΦ Declared panel type: growable axes, size limits, optional ParamSchema for the editor.
- `HudResizeAxes` (type): type HudResizeAxes = "none" | "x" | "y" | "both" έΑΦ Which axes a panel type may grow when resized in canvas mode. Resize is semantic έΑΦ content reflows (longer track, more rows) έΑΦ never a CSS scale of the whole panel.
- `ResolvedHudPanelLayout` (interface): interface ResolvedHudPanelLayout έΑΦ Fully resolved panel layout after document + fallback merge.
- `listHudPanelTypes` (function): function listHudPanelTypes(): HudPanelTypeDef[] έΑΦ Every registered panel type, sorted by id έΑΦ editor palette / agent listing.
- `registerHudPanelType` (function): function registerHudPanelType(def: HudPanelTypeDef): void έΑΦ Register a HUD panel type so the editor can list/add/configure it and canvas resize knows axes.
- `resizePanelSize` (function): function resizePanelSize(current: HudSize, delta: { dw: number; dh: number }, axes: HudResizeAxes, limits?: { minWidth?: number; maxWidth?: number; minHeight?: number; maxHeight?: number; }): HudSize έΑΦ Semantic resize: apply pixel deltas only on growable axes, clamped to min/max. Never scales content έΑΦ callers reflow layout size (track length, list rows).
- `resolveHudPanelLayout` (function): function resolveHudPanelLayout(docPanel: EditorUiPanelLayout | undefined, fallback: HudPanelFallback): ResolvedHudPanelLayout έΑΦ Resolve a panel's layout: document entry wins field-by-field over TSX fallback. TSX props are fallback-only έΑΦ the scene document is the source of truth once authored.

- `HudAnchor` (type): type HudAnchor = | "top-left" | "top" | "top-right" | "left" | "center" | "right" | "bottom-left" | "bottom" | "bottom-right" έΑΦ έγι undocumented
